paper_claim: Hybrid LSTM-GARCH+VIX significantly outperforms LSTM-GARCH (no VIX) and pure GARCH; ML approaches significantly beat GARCH baseline

reproduction_setup.train_test_split: 60/40 (3317 train, 2212 test) - approximation of paper's walk-forward
models: GARCH(1,1) with periodic refit (every 20 days) and manual recursion update between refits
LSTM (1 layer, 50 hidden, lookback=20, features: log_ret + sq_ret)
approximation_notes: Paper uses walk-forward (rolling refit each step); we use 60/40 split with periodic refits.
LSTM hyperparameters set to standard defaults; paper does not disclose its exact spec.
GARCH variance update uses manual recursion sigma2 = omega + alpha*r^2 + beta*sigma2_prev between refits.

dm_winner_vs_runner_up.interpretation: Paper's headline claim that adding VIX significantly improves the LSTM-GARCH hybrid is NOT supported.

dm_winner_vs_garch_baseline.interpretation: Paper's claim that ML hybrids beat plain GARCH is NOT supported at 5%.

mcs_at_90pct.interpretation: All 4 models tie at 90% MCS; no statistically distinguishable winner.

spa_winner_as_benchmark.interpretation: SPA cannot establish LSTM_GARCH_VIX as significantly the best.

phase2_summary_for_this_paper.headline_finding: Paper P26's claim that 'LSTM-GARCH+VIX significantly outperforms' is NOT supported by significance testing in our reproduction. DM(VIX hybrid vs no-VIX hybrid) p=0.37; DM(VIX hybrid vs plain GARCH) p=0.20; MCS at 90% retains all 4 models.
phase2_summary_for_this_paper.what_this_means: Adding VIX to a hybrid LSTM-GARCH does not improve forecasts in our reproduction — it actually does slightly worse than the no-VIX hybrid by mean QLIKE, and the difference is not significant. The paper's strong claim is an artifact of point-estimate ranking without confidence intervals.
